methodology: Cross-reference adjacency matrix (REV.14.3→REV.5.9 = 10v strongest link) + patristic evidence (19 citations: Augustine on every psalm, Clement of Alexandria 'Christ IS the New Song', Bede hear-vs-utter distinction, Ephrem gentile argument) + CCEL commentaries (Calvin, Henry, Barnes, JFB) + topics (SONG, PRAISE, MUSIC)

evidence: Catena Bible (Augustine 'grace of faith', Haydock 'canticle of the Lamb, Apocalypse')
Catena Bible (Augustine 'singing IS building the house of God; cites Jn 13:34 new commandment')
CCEL (Calvin 'universal not just Israel', Henry 'NT song eternal never grows old' cites Rev 14:3)
Catena Bible (Augustine 'old man=old song; new man=new song; love NEVER grows old; who separates from saints sings old song')

relevance_change.reason: Augustine extensive commentary on old/new man = old/new song. Love never grows old. 5v→REV.14.3. Calvin alludes to Is 42:10. Was missing score — assigned 2.

evidence: Catena Bible (Clement of Alexandria 'CHRIST IS the New Song — the Logos manifested in flesh = the Song', Ephrem 'gentiles with MORE reason')
CCEL (Calvin 'cannot be sung but by renewed men', Henry, JFB 'never sung before')

relevance_change.reason: Prophetic source within 1st Servant Song (42:1-9). Clement of Alexandria: 'Christ IS the New Song' — most radical christological identification in the research. Calvin anticipates Rev 14:3 logic: 'cannot be sung but by renewed men.'

existing_refs_confirmed.justification: Prophetic source within 1st Servant Song (42:1-9). Clement's christological identification is the most radical in the entire research. Calvin anticipates Rev 14:3 logic.

evidence: Catena Bible (Bede 'NT sacrament complete in Christ', 'living creatures+elders = the Church')
CCEL (Barnes 'new because consequent on redemption' cites Ps40:3,96:1,144:9,Is42:10,Rev14:3)
Catena Bible (Bede 'elect can HEAR but not UTTER the song; cites Is54:1,56:5')
CCEL (JFB 'as new as it is old as God's eternal purpose')

new_refs_added.justification: UNIQUE: only verse where GOD PLACES the song in the mouth (not a command to sing). 5v←REV.5.9, 4v←ISA.42.10. Augustine: Christ speaks as Head of the Body. Henry: applicable to Christ's resurrection/exaltation. Topic SONG: 'A new'. Barnes cites in Rev 5:9. Anticipates Rev 14:3 (song as gift, not effort).

Typological synthesis: song of Moses AND the Lamb = unifies OT and NT. 4v←REV.14.3, 7v←EXO.15.1. Does NOT contain 'new song' phrase — valuable semantic test (lexical search misses, semantic should find). Topic SONG: 'Of Moses and the Lamb'. Topic MUSIC: 'In heaven'.

candidates_noted.justification: Nearly identical to Ps 96:1 but with reason clause ('because he has done marvels'). Augustine: 'holy arm = Christ' (cites Is 53:1). 5v←PSA.96.1, 4v←REV.14.3. Marginal addition over Ps 96:1.
Davidic/royal 'new song' with psaltery of 10 strings. Augustine: '10 strings = 10 commandments; who has no love can CARRY the psaltery but cannot SING'. 3v←PSA.33.3, 4v←REV.14.3.

scholarly_notes.christological_identification: Clement of Alexandria (AD 215) on Is 42:10: 'Christ IS the New Song — the Logos manifested in flesh = the Song itself.' The most radical christological claim in the entire validation corpus.
scholarly_notes.augustine_framework: Augustine comments on EVERY psalm containing 'new song' (Ps 33, 40, 96, 98, 144, 149) with consistent framework: old man=old song, new man=new song, OT=old song, NT=new song. Love never grows old → song always new. Singing = building God's house.
scholarly_notes.hear_vs_utter: Bede on Rev 14:3: the elect can HEAR the song but not UTTER it. Exclusivity is of EXPERIENCE, not salvation. The 144,000 have unique proximity to the Lamb.
scholarly_notes.redemptive_arc: 7-stage arc: (1) Psalms: each deliverance→new praise, (2) Ps 40:3: God PLACES the song, (3) Is 42:10: Servant brings 'new things'→unprecedented song, (4) Clement: Christ=Song, (5) Rev 5:9: Lamb slain→universal redemption→defined content, (6) Rev 14:3: experiential exclusivity, (7) Rev 15:3: synthesis Moses+Lamb.
